commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(ai-chat): Wire RAG tool call to middleware /retrieve endpoint (ETC-479)

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: refactor(ai-chat): Remove unused non-streaming AI response path (ETC-481)
commits.body:
Remove provide_ai_response and its entire chain: REST route, AiService
method, OpenAiResponseParser, interface method, fake provider method,
frontend API client method, and all associated tests. Only the streaming
path (stream_ai_response) remains.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(ai-chat): Register RAG tool call with mocked results (ETC-478)
commits.body:
When ENABLE_AI_RAG is on, the AI chat registers a rag_retrieve function
tool that replaces web_search. The model can call it to retrieve docs,
which currently returns hardcoded mocked results. A follow-up request
sends the results back to OpenAI so the AI can reference them in its
response. WideEventLogger observability tracks RAG/web_search state and
queries.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(ai-rag): Call middleware /health from plugin (ETC-476)
commits.body:
Add ENABLE_AI_RAG feature flag, REST proxy route for middleware
health check, and frontend wiring that console.logs the result
on store init.

commits.author: Matteo Greco
commits.subject: feat(skills): Add release-announcement skill [hide] [skip ci]
commits.body:
Fetches a GitHub release by version tag, consolidates and rewrites
changelog items for readability, categorizes them as New/Improve/Fix,
and generates a short user-facing announcement summary.

ETC-479.title: Plugin: wire tool call callback to middleware /retrieve
ETC-479.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

* RagToolHandler contacts the middleware's `POST /retrieve` endpoint instead of using its own hardcoded mock data
* Returns `WP_Error` on network failure, auth failure (401/403), rate limit (429), timeout, malformed response
* Chat works identically to today when middleware is unreachable or times out
* No user-visible error when RAG returns empty results — AI answers without extra context
* Tested: successful call, network failure, timeout, malformed response

ETC-481.title: Plugin: Remove unused non-streaming AI response path
ETC-481.description:
## Context

The non-streaming `provide_ai_response` method in `OpenAiProvider` is no longer called by the frontend — all AI chat uses `stream_ai_response`. The old path still has a route, callback, response parser, and tests.

## Scope

* Remove `provide_ai_response` from `OpenAiProvider` (and from `AiProviderInterface` if defined there)
* Remove the corresponding REST API route and callback
* Remove `OpenAiResponseParser` if it's only used by the non-streaming path
* Remove associated tests
* Verify no other code references the removed method/route

## Acceptance Criteria

* Non-streaming AI route no longer exists
* Streaming AI chat continues to work as before
* No dead code remains

ETC-478.title: Plugin: register RAG tool call with mocked results
ETC-478.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

* When `ENABLE_AI_RAG` is on, a RAG retrieval tool call is registered in the AI chat payload
* The tool call callback returns hardcoded/mocked results (does not call the middleware yet)
* AI receives the mocked results and can reference them in its response
* When `ENABLE_AI_RAG` is off, chat works identically to today — from [ETC-458](https://linear.app/digital-gravy/issue/ETC-458/fall-back-to-web-search-when-rag-is-unavailable-or-returns-no-results)
* Tested: tool call is present when flag is on, absent when off; callback returns expected shape

ETC-476.title: Plugin: call middleware /health
ETC-476.description:
## Acceptance Criteria

* Hide RAG feature behind `ENABLE_AI_RAG` feature flag (off by default)
* Plugin calls `GET /health` on the middleware when the feature flag is on
* Response is logged to the browser console
* No user-visible UI change — this is a proof-of-wiring step
